items.briefing:
Good news for everyday Australians: cybercrime risk targeting individuals has reportedly decreased, however pressure is shifting toward small and medium businesses (SMBs), which are now increasingly in attackers' sights. Separately, a new study finds most organisations have already experienced AI-related security incidents, a timely reminder as AI tools become more common in workplaces including RTOs.

Critical software vulnerabilities are also increasing rapidly, with new research showing exposures have doubled. Keep your devices and applications updated promptly — if you receive a prompt to update software today, don't postpone it.

featured.title: Aussies Face Reduced Cybercrime Risk, as Pressure Shifts to SMBs
featured.quote: Cybercrime risk shifts from large institutions to vulnerable Australian SMBs lacking resources.
